properties.evidence: In a meta-analysis of 38,153 statin treated subjects in whom non-HDL-C, LDL-C and Apo-B were compared, all three markers were predictive of CVD, with non-HDL-C showing a slightly greater association than the other two. However, even though statistically non-HDL-C was the best predictor, the 95% confidence intervals of the hazard ratio of CVD for each of the 3 markers overlapped sufficiently that this study cannot be considered conclusive.

rejected_candidates.text: In a simplified version, the catabolic steps can be thought of as a conversion of VLDL to intermediate density lipoproteins (IDL), which are then converted to LDL. The particles are defined by their different densities and TG:Cholesterol ratios, but all of them contain apoB-100.
rejected_candidates.reason: The text does not explicitly support any relationship between entities.

rejected_candidates.text: In the case of chylomicrons, which transport dietary fat, the end product of TG lipolysis is a small, dense Apo-B48 containing particle known as a chylomicron remnant.
